rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HSU VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 3, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Hearing required concerning the January 6, 2026 verifications. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL CHAN VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys? Fees, Costs And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SAMUEL CHAN's Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses. (Complete tentative ruling e-mailed to the parties). Plaintiff Samuel Chan moves for an award of attorneys fees following his settlement of Song-Beverly Act litigation against Ford Motor Company. The court awards fees of $23,488 plus costs in the amount of $5764.60 for the reasons stated herein. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: PAWNEE LEASING CORPORATION VS. DION HEALTH MANAGEMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 5, DEFENDANT AMIN SAMADIAN's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment. Defendant Amin Samadian's motion to vacate entry of default and default judgment is denied. Samadian contends that he did not receive personal service or actual notice of this lawsuit. The process server's declaration states that Samadian was personally served at his business address on January 22, 2025. Samadian provides a declaration stating that he was not present at that address on that day and did not personally receive the summons and complaint. (Samadian Decl., para. 5.) The court does not find the Samadian Declaration to be credible: it states that Samadian "did not learn that a default and default judgment had been entered until that December 2025 date." (Samadian Decl., para. 13.) But plaintiff Pawnee Leasing Corporation provides evidence that Samadian was in correspondence with its attorney about the judgment in September 2025. (Adams Decl., Ex. 1.) Because the court credits the declaration of the registered process server and does not credit the Samadian Declaration, it concludes that the plaintiff has carried its burden of showing that service was proper. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. VS. BUMBLE BEE FOODS, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Separate Trial On "Safe Harbor" Defense And To Stay Of All Other Aspects Of The Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2026, Line 7, DEFENDANT BUMBLE BEE FOODS, LLC's Motion For Separate Trial On "Safe Harbor" Defense And To Stay Of All Other Aspects Of The Case. The motion for a separate trial on defendant Bumble Bee Foods, LLC's "safe harbor" defense, is denied without prejudice. This court uses a master calendar system, and the law and motion department does not bifurcate cases that are sent out to other judges for trial. Defendant may seek single assignment from the presiding judge and this motion may be made to the assigned trial judge if single assignment is granted. The motion to stay all aspects of the case until trial on the "safe harbor" defense is denied; the request for stay is premised on the request for bifurcation. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA).
